note: Black knight on e5 is undefended; Rxe5 wins it cleanly (SEE +320).
Nf6+ forks the g8 king and the e8 rook; f6 is safe.
Black knight on e7 is absolutely pinned to the e8 king by Re1; taking it loses the exchange so it is not 'hanging'.
Re8# — the f7/g7/h7 pawns box the king on h8.
Quiet open position after 1.e4 e5 — no capture, fork, pin, or back-rank threat.
Symmetric four-knights — Nxe5 is defended (an equal trade, not a win), so nothing should fire.
